suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The rust-library-worker skill should explicitly support review-only or evidence-only cleanup tasks where runtime behavior is already correct and existing focused regressions already prove it.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both delete cleanup and create_at protocol cleanup reviews noted justified deviations because the current skill rigidly requires a failing regression first even when the task is commit-scope cleanup or trait-surface narrowing already covered by existing tests.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The rust-library-worker skill should add concrete guidance for detached-head or dirty-tree commit hazards so workers know how to avoid capturing unrelated mission artifacts in feature commits.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The original delete-string feature failed review because commit 16e1f07 included unrelated validation artifact deletions, and the prior review traced this to weak guidance around recovering from unrelated tracked changes during commit creation.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Review prompts or shared guidance should clarify where repo-local skill files live when user-level skill paths are absent.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The create_at removal review noted that repo-local .factory skill files existed while the generic reviewer expectation for a user-level skill path did not; clarifying this would reduce avoidable review friction.

rejectedObservations.observation: GitNexus tooling was unavailable in one worker session and the worker fell back to source inspection plus git diff review.
